claimText: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is conducting pilot projects for two distinct types of Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs): retail and wholesale.
evidenceExcerpt: The RBI is currently conducting pilot projects of two types of CBDCs – retail and wholesale.
examUse: Statement-based question on the types of CBDC pilots initiated by the RBI.
distractorHooks: Claiming RBI is only piloting retail CBDC
Claiming SEBI or NPCI is conducting the CBDC pilot projects instead of RBI

claimText: Retail CBDC is designed for use by the general public and is accessible via digital wallets and smartphone apps.
evidenceExcerpt: Retail CBDC: It is available to the general public via digital wallets, smartphone apps, etc.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question defining the target users and accessibility of Retail CBDC.
distractorHooks: Stating Retail CBDC is restricted to retail banks and merchants
Claiming Retail CBDC requires specialized hardware tokens rather than standard smartphone apps

claimText: Wholesale CBDC is utilized among banks and other licensed financial institutions specifically for interbank payments and securities transactions.
evidenceExcerpt: Wholesale CBDCs: Used among banks and other licensed financial institutions for interbank payments and securities transactions.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing the use-cases of Wholesale CBDC versus Retail CBDC.
distractorHooks: Swapping the definition with Retail CBDC
Claiming Wholesale CBDC is used by large corporations for bulk public transactions
Stating Wholesale CBDC is used for international remittances by the general public
